nativeRange.habitat: Sand-rubble bottoms adjacent to reefs in shallow coastal waters, typically 0–10 m depth

diet.notes: Generally accepts a wide range of small meaty foods including live and frozen brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, live blackworms, and prepared carnivore foods. Multiple small feedings per day (at least twice daily) are commonly recommended.

avoidWith: other Jaguar Gobies in small tanks

summary: A small, peaceful reef-safe goby from the Indo-West Pacific with a beige body marked by variable black bands. Generally considered a good fit for nano and established reef aquariums; available as captive-bred from ORA.
careNotes:
The Jaguar Goby (Gobiopsis quinquecincta) is a small reef-associated goby native to sand-rubble habitats throughout the Indo-West Pacific, including Sri Lanka, the Andaman Sea, Malaysia, the Philippines, Palau, and Japan. The common name 'Jaguar Goby' is a trade designation; FishBase uses the common name 'Fiveband Barbelgoby' in reference to the species' chin and snout barbels and the four to five dark vertical bands along the body.

It is generally considered an easy beginner saltwater fish due to its peaceful temperament, small adult size, and reef compatibility. A well-established aquarium with plenty of live rock arranged to form crevices and tunnels, plus an open sand or rubble bed, tends to suit it well. The species naturally seeks out small crevices that it will defend as a personal territory.

Although peaceful toward other species, it has been reported to be territorial toward conspecifics, especially in small aquaria. It is generally best kept singly unless the tank is large enough to give each individual its own crevice. The species was introduced to the captive-bred market by ORA (Oceans, Reefs & Aquariums), who first acquired broodstock in late 2010; aquacultured specimens are widely available.

Like most gobies, this species is considered a jumping risk and a tightly fitted lid or screen top is generally advised to prevent escape.

Adult-size reports differ between scientific and hobby sources: FishBase lists a wild maximum of 2.4 cm SL, while aquarium trade sources (LiveAquaria, ORA, Reef Builders, TankFacts) commonly cite 1.5–3 inches (approx. 3.8–7.6 cm) in captivity. The larger captive sizes likely reflect total length (including caudal fin) and well-fed aquacultured specimens. Water-parameter ranges were averaged across LiveAquaria (72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025) and TankFacts (76–80°F, pH 7.8–8.4, SG 1.023–1.026); FishBase's wild temperature range (24.7–29.3°C) is broader on the warm side and consistent with the species' tropical/subtropical distribution.

Minimum tank size is widely reported by aquarium retailers as around 10 gallons (~38 L), reflecting this species' content disposition in nano reefs. The 75 L floor used here follows the project's standardized minimum-tank ladder and provides additional swimming and territory room without being inconsistent with the species' actual space requirements.
breedingNotes: Captive-bred by ORA since approximately 2010; commercial aquaculture demonstrates that the species can be successfully reared, though specific home-aquarium spawning protocols are not widely documented in popular care literature.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary trade source. The collection URL listed in the manifest (https://www.liveaquaria.com/collections/gobies) did not include the Jaguar Goby; the species' dedicated product page was located via web search.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used for taxonomy confirmation (Gobiidae, Gobiiformes), scientific common name (Fiveband Barbelgoby), native range (Indo-West Pacific), habitat (sand-rubble bottoms adjacent to reefs, 0–10 m depth), and wild temperature range (24.7–29.3°C). Cited wild max length is 2.4 cm SL, notably smaller than captive-grown specimens.

additional.name: ORA (Oceans, Reefs & Aquariums)
additional.notes: Captive-bred source. Confirmed scientific name, adult size (up to 3 in / ~7.6 cm in captivity), peaceful temperament with conspecific territoriality, reef safety, and dietary flexibility.

additional.name: Reef Builders
additional.notes: Confirmed native range details (Sri Lanka, Palau, Malaysia, Philippines, Andaman Sea, Japan), depth range (0–10 m), ORA captive-breeding history (broodstock acquired late 2010), and behavior.

additional.name: TankFacts
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters (76–80°F, pH 7.8–8.4, SG 1.023–1.026), captive size (up to 3 in), peaceful temperament, conspecific territoriality, and jumping risk.

fish.breedingNotes: Successfully bred at commercial scale by ORA; not commonly bred in home aquaria. No standard home-spawning protocol is documented.
